audit_summary: Woodfire Westport has a strong public reputation with 573 reviews and a 4.6-star rating, but the website does not convert that goodwill into direct reservations or enquiries. The absence of a booking system and contact form, combined with a single-page static layout and moderate mobile performance, means the site is functioning as a passive brochure rather than an active sales channel. Focused conversion improvements and mobile optimisation would have an immediate and measurable impact.

performance: Mobile PageSpeed score of 74 is moderate, indicating room for improvement on handheld devices where the majority of restaurant discovery searches take place.
Desktop PageSpeed score of 99 is strong, confirming the core build is technically sound and mobile gains are achievable without a full rebuild.
Possible causes of the mobile gap include unoptimised images, render-blocking scripts, or third-party embeds, though these are flagged as possible only based on the score differential.
mobile_ux: Mobile UX is rated ok, which suggests the site is functional but not optimised for the mobile-first behaviour of restaurant visitors.
Tap targets, font sizing, and content hierarchy should be reviewed to ensure the experience is frictionless for users arriving from Google Maps or social media on a mobile device.
With no booking or contact action available, mobile visitors currently have no clear next step beyond browsing, which is a significant missed conversion opportunity.
conversion: No booking system is present. This is the most significant conversion gap for a hospitality business with an established reputation and high review volume.
No contact form is present. Visitors who wish to enquire about group bookings, events, or dietary requirements have no on-site pathway to do so.
No click-to-call signal was provided. A prominent phone number with click-to-call functionality should be confirmed or added as a minimum conversion fallback.
Single-page layout limits the ability to guide users through a structured journey from discovery to reservation.
No online ordering capability is visible, which may represent an additional revenue stream depending on the business model.
seo_fundamentals: No blog or updates section is present, limiting the site's ability to generate fresh content signals and rank for long-tail local search queries over time.
Single-page static structure provides minimal content depth, reducing the number of indexable pages and topical signals available to search engines.
No dedicated about section is noted, which limits the opportunity to reinforce local identity, team story, and brand trust signals that support local SEO.
Schema markup for restaurant, local business, and menu would be recommended to improve search result appearance and local intent matching, though current implementation is unknown.
Location and service context appear limited on-page based on the notes provided, which may reduce visibility for location-qualified searches such as restaurant Westport or wood-fired pizza Mayo.
trust_social_proof: Reviews rating of 4.6 across 573 Google reviews is a strong trust asset that is not currently being leveraged on the website itself.
No testimonials integration is noted on the site, meaning visitors arriving directly to the website do not see social proof unless they navigate away to Google.
Embedding a curated selection of Google reviews or a live review widget would significantly strengthen on-site credibility and support conversion.
Social links presence is unknown based on the data provided.
security_maintenance: SSL is active, which is a positive baseline for visitor trust and search ranking.
The site runs on WordPress, which benefits from regular maintenance to keep themes, plugins, and core files current and performing optimally.
A proactive maintenance plan is recommended to ensure continued performance, uptime reliability, and compatibility as the site grows.

p1_quick_wins: Add a table booking system or reservation widget, such as OpenTable, ResDiary, or a simple enquiry form, to create a direct conversion pathway for visitors arriving with intent to dine.
Embed a curated Google reviews section or live review widget on the homepage to surface the 4.6-star reputation directly on-site and reinforce trust at the point of decision.
Ensure a click-to-call phone number is prominently displayed above the fold on mobile, providing an immediate fallback action for visitors not ready to use a booking form.
p2_growth_improvements: Optimise mobile performance to push the PageSpeed score above 80 by compressing images, deferring non-critical scripts, and reviewing any third-party embeds that may be adding load time.
Expand the single-page layout to include a dedicated about section and a menu page, increasing content depth and providing additional indexable surfaces for local search.
Implement restaurant and local business schema markup to improve how the site appears in search results and support Google Maps integration with structured data.
p3_strategic_enhancements: Introduce a simple news or updates section to publish seasonal menus, events, or local content, building ongoing search relevance and giving returning visitors a reason to re-engage.
Develop a location-focused content strategy targeting high-intent queries such as dining in Westport, best restaurants Co. Mayo, and wood-fired pizza Westport to grow organic visibility over time.

assumptions_and_unknowns: Hosting provider is unknown and has not been assessed.
Click-to-call presence on mobile was not confirmed in the provided data.
Social media profiles and links were not provided and have not been evaluated.
Online ordering feasibility depends on kitchen operations and has not been assumed as a requirement.
Schema markup implementation status is unknown and has been recommended rather than confirmed as missing.
Third-party integrations or plugins in use are unknown and have not been assessed.
Whether a phone number is present on the site was not confirmed in the input data.
next_best_action: Share a short two to three point improvement plan focused on adding a booking pathway and surfacing the existing review reputation on-site, and propose a 15-minute call to walk through how these changes could be implemented quickly with minimal disruption to the current site.
